desc:
This kitchen has every utensil there is, with the wall covered with shelves full
of pots, jars, sets of knives, spoons and forks, glassware, plates and all the
other things a cook might need. The wooden floor has a patch of stone just in
front of the stove to prevent fires from starting. A small well is actually
drilled in the northeastern corner, near the sink.
name: Luxurious Kitchen

desc:
In this room dark wood has been used for floor, walls and furniture. Bright
coloured cloth cover the sofas, armchairs, tables and walls though, lighting it
up a little. This room must have been intended for recreation, relaxing, and
learned discussions. A pair of large thick oaken doors lead north.
name: Livingroom

desc:
This is a large room, almost thirty yards long and twenty yards wide. A trampled
barren earthfloor and wooden walls that lean inwards on the eastern, western and
northern sides surround a large platform made of stone near the northern end.
Low wooden benches stand at both sides of a path leading up to the platform, so
this must once have been some kind of meeting hall. The architecture here is
different from the one used in the house itself, and this part is probably the
older. A small balcony hangs out from the southern wall about ten yards up, and
an archway seems to lead back into the building. A thick rope runs from this
ledge over the platform and ends in the northern wall, and a basket of sorts
hangs down from the rope above the platform.
name: In a Wooden Dome

desc:
Coming into this room you can barely make out where the walls are. This area is
long and narrow and you can barely see in front of you. With all the things
crawling around it may be best if you light a torch or something and see where
it is you are heading. This may had been a mistake to enter this cave. Water
drips from the ceiling falling onto your head sending chills down your back.
name: A Small Water Cave

desc:
In the cave is nothing, there looks to be a few holes that lead no where along
here in the walls. There is nothing but what feels like sticks laying in here,
stumbling along in here may not be a good idea, dead ends go in all directions
and getting lost in here could be easy to do.
name: A Dark, Damp Water Cave

desc:
The cave at this part of the area is losing its light from the above ground.
Going further into this area is going to be a blinding experience. There will be
no light from here on out and this coolness of the cave can be felt already on
your skin. A faint sound of water can be heard coming from somewhere. A slimy
feel along the ground is also there.
name: A Small Water Cave

desc:
A smelly, damp cave lies dark here. A large stone door was once set in the
northern wall but it lies broken on the floor now. Some old rags and rubble
cover the floors, the walls being cracked and broken.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
In the middle of this room a large pile of crystal shards glows sickly green. It
is surrounded by a chaotic mass of broken items, splinters, parts of furniture.
Everything is devastated, ruined, crushed and ripped apart. A large stone door
lies cracked in five parts at the entrance to the northern cave.
name: Devastated Cave

desc:
What once must have been a dwarven home is now just rubble and wreckage. Ripped
apart tapestries, a broken desk, fragments of weapons and wooden splinters cover
the cracked rock floor. At some places it looks as if a giant has pounded on the
walls with his bare fists, leaving huge cracks and marks. Some water is running
along the small clefts in the ceiling, making the air in here cold and moist.
name: Among the Rubble

desc:
Narrowly bounded by a thick barrier of woody undergrowth and a steep cliff, this
north-west passage links the open plains to the south to the heart of the wild
brushland to the north. Even at the edge of the bushes, visibility is low, and
old trunks and broken branches make for uneven footing.
name: Edge of the Brush

desc:
Deep in the brush the bushes grow denser, having grown isolated in this place
for a very long time. Long thorns and snarled vines are everpresent, and the
ground is uneven where titanic roots crawl through the earth. Here and there,
trees pierce through the top of the gigantic thornbushes.
name: Brushland of Fornost

desc:
Massive trees of all kinds loom sporadically above thick, thorny bushes,
crowding the view for what must be miles. The heart of the brushland is old and
wild, thick with plant life and filled with the faint chirps and deep growling
of wildlife.
name: Dense Bushes

desc:
There is a streambed that generally flows from west to south. The streambed
still carries some water toward the south despite being dry to the west. Only
swampy ground remains on the borders of the streambed's dried-up sections. The
grass is short and the terrain is flat. A path through the area is indicated by
a few rocks laying on the damp grass.
name: Dry Streambed on a Boggy Land

desc:
The ground seems fertile and the grass is high with some big rocks scattered
over the land. Most of the rocks are covered with moss, which is a sign that
travellers do not pass too often. From here it is possible to see the
surroundings quite well. While the ground is just marshy to the west and north,
the south opens up to wide rocky plateaus.
name: Fork in the Waterway

desc:
The rocky plateaus are covered with ash. A creek connects a brown pond to the
north, where it appears that the grounds are more fertile and attractive.
Further travel is impossible to the west due to muddy ponds that appear to be
dangerous and emit a strong sulphurous scent. There are extensive, arid plains
to the south.
name: Small, Murky Creek

desc:
The burned ground is uneven and rocky in spots with long dry tufts of grass
covering the remainder. The surroundings are black, looking as if burned down by
fire. With the exception of the north, where wide rocky plateaus appear, the
plains extend in all directions.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
The rocky surface of this burned plains looks sharp and dangerous. There is a
trail leading toward a sketchy-looking small hut to the west at the edge of a
cliff. To the north and south wide barren plains of burned land continue far
toward the horizon.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
This area shows fewer signs of life and more of draught and malnourishment. The
burned plains are covered with ash and dust continuing toward the north. Toward
the west a sandy slope descends to a great open space while toward the south the
plains slowly descend to fertile lowlands.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
The plains continue to slightly descend into the lowlands toward the south.
While these lands look healthy and offer enough food for animals the land gets
more desolate with every step taken toward the northern direction where the
green grass turns into brown and unhealthy-looking lands.
name: Lowland Plain

desc:
The fertile lowlands are covered with deep green grass and occasional bushes.
This is the perfect spot for grazing animals. Far toward the northwest the lands
ascends gradually from a healthy green-looking landscape toward a desolate
brown.
name: Fertile Lowland Plain

desc:
Deep green grass grows on the fertile lowlands in every direction making this
the perfect environment for grazing animals. More trees and bushes seem to grow
toward the southern direction while to the west the grass gets a little bit
higher making it harder to travel.
name: Lowland Plain

desc:
Lush green grass grows beside the brook, looking very inviting for a place to
rest. The sand on the bottom of the brook is dark, making it hard to see what
might hide beneath the surface. Thorny bushes make travel east and south
impossible.
name: Brookside Brush

desc:
In this dark cave the damp walls are rough in texture and have many tiny flecks
and minerals embedded in them. The smell of stagnant water makes it hard to
breathe. A small path winds its way up between rocks and boulders until it meets
dense bushes.
name: Dark Subterranean Cave

desc:
A large cavern is filled with stagnant murky water. Several parts of the ceiling
hang low over the surface and leave only a small space to navigate on the lakes
surface. A small rocky underground beach to the west seems to be the only part
of this cavern that isn't flooded with water.
name: Subterranean Lake

desc:
The grey rocks below the lake's surface have depth, and the water gives them a
strong tint. The boulder in the brine waits patiently for all eternity because
there is nothing but chilly water around it. There is absolutely no current in
the cold water, and there are no obvious ways to keep diving rather than
ascending to the surface.
name: Dark Underwater Cave

desc:
A narrow tunnel dug into the earth descends slightly to the south leading deeper
under the ground. The tunnel runs in a general north to south direction and
seems to be artificially designed. Toward the north the tunnel ascends until it
ends abruptly at a ladder leading straight up into a hole overgrown with vines
and branches.
name: Dead-end in a Narrowing Tunnel

desc:
Groundwater drips down the dirt walls that are supported by rotting timber,
trickling quietly into a small pool, before flowing out by some hidden passage.
The air is damp with the smell of decaying wood, and the tunnel floor is covered
with a mix of rock and mud.
name: Pool of Water

desc:
This narrow, mouldy tunnel has been dug haphazardly through the earth, with
crude support beams providing a modicum of protection against collapse. A
trickle of water dribbles northward, creating a small depression through the
muddy floor. Twisted roots hang in the air below the ceiling, their journey
through the earth ending in this tunnel.
name: Damp, Narrowing Tunnel

desc:
Strong roots binding the dirt walls and roof overhead assist half-rotten timber
supports, preventing this tunnel from collapsing. The air within this tunnel, to
the north, smells mouldy and damp, and a quiet trickle of water permeates the
darkness.
name: Entrance to a Damp Tunnel

desc:
The interior of this room looks dusty and unused. Ancient furnishings
reminiscent of Arda's Second Age are handsomely placed about the room. Several
paintings and tapestries depict cruel battle scenes while one painting shows a
red-robed man with a mocking smile. A huge bookshelf, which looks as if it was
torn from the wall is laying in a cluttered heap on the floor. The space is lit
softly by torches that are mounted on the stone walls.
name: Ground Floor of a Tower

desc:
This room is surprisingly wide and spacious. A thin window in the south wall
allows for sunlight during the day however the large room leaves enough shadow
to stay out of the sun. The space is lit by candles set on the stone walls.
Lavish decor dominates the setting while long dark green velvet drapes line the
windows. The large bed pushed against the far wall is made from finely crafted
stone. A huge mirror with silver wrought around its edges in delicate designs is
secured to the east wall. A small staircase allows access to the ground floor
and the battlements to the top.
name: Lavish Bedchamber

desc:
The top of the tower measures about thirty feet from one end to the other. Its
edges are secured by battlements twisted in shape and design. The only way to
exit the top of the tower is a fairly large trapdoor in one corner through which
it is possible to access a staircase.
name: Battlements of a Tower

desc:
The barren pasture's ground is covered with dry grass and occasional bushes. To
the west, the pasture is limited by a long stone wall which is set up to protect
animals from whatever danger might be on the other side of the wall. To the
north, the backside of a house made out of white stone is built right into the
stone wall with a large veranda facing the pasture.
name: Barren Pasture

desc:
The pasture slightly ascends and bends to the east. A long stone wall is set up
to protect animals from the steep drops in the terrain to the south and west
side. The ground is covered with large natural rocks and boulders, rare patches
of grass and bushes between them.
name: Depositioned Pasture

desc:
The pasture is very exposed to winds on this side of the elevation. Wind-torn
grass and small strangely bent shrubs and bushes cover the ground. The small
path through the pasture bends from the west and ascends slightly toward the
north. A long stone wall protects animals from steep drops to the east and
south.
name: Exposed Pasture

desc:
At the top of this slight rise, the narrow route that led up the grassland comes
to an end. Most of the bushes around the crest look either dead or sick.
Although the land is grim, there is no abundance of small animal life. West of
the crest the vegetation seems to be a bit denser just before a long stone wall
is set up to protect animals in all directions but south.
name: Brush among Rocks

desc:
This wooden platform is several dozen feet above the ground of what seems to be
a natural cavern. Below the platform a wooden construction is set up against the
eastern wall which can be used to climb down to the ground of the cavern. The
ceiling above the platform is stable rock and a small ladder leads further up
toward a hatch.
name: Escape Platform

desc:
This natural cavern seems to be well maintained. The walls are lined with
torch-holders and to the west the floor and walls are clad with wooden planks.
Several straw mats indicate that this place is used as a sleeping place. The
cavern has a very high ceiling and an improvised wooden construction has been
setup at the eastern wall to reach a platform dozens of feet above the ground.
name: Seedy Cavern

desc:
Wooden planks cover the floor and parts of the walls of this artificially
crafted tunnel which seems to be used as a hideout. Along the walls there are
cabinets and shelves which seem to be used to store goods. In the centre of the
room a wooden stair leads up to a trapdoor while to the north and east there are
passages to other rooms. To provide adequate light to move through the tunnel,
torches are set on the walls.
name: Seedy Hideout

desc:
This underground room seems to be the main common room. A large table and
several chairs around it serve as a place of relaxation. Mounted on the walls
are several pegs and shelves which are used to store stuff. At the far side of
the room a large map of the nearby vicinity covers the wall. The only visible
exit is a passage to the south. A number of candles that have been placed on the
large table light the space.
name: Seedy Hideout

desc:
This meadow is filled with large snarled bushes, growing densely all tangled
together. The ground is barely visible beneath all the vegetation and where it
is, it is instead covered by gnarled roots. Towards the south the bushes thin
out and are instead replaced by flowering rose bushes.
name: Tangled Bushes

desc:
Several large stands of hazel dot a landscape otherwise covered by thriving
bushes. Large and small bushes spread out wide branches in all directions,
hiding the ground completely. Anything might be hidden from view beneath the
dense greenery, and the occasional rustling suggests that there's plenty of
animal life.
name: Tangled Bushes

desc:
Great snarls of roots cover the ground, twice or thrice as thick as the stems of
the bushes they originate from, whose snagging, bristly thorns further inhibit
travel. There is a constant carpet of background sound: chirping, hissing, and
rustling that suggests a rich fauna.
name: Tangled Bushes

desc:
Sprawling wild rose bushes dominate the scenery here, growing so fiercly that
nary a route is available in-between them. Long, nasty-looking thorns protect
the pink and red flowers, and the air is full of insects. Towards the north the
rose bushes thin out, only to be replaced by other species of sprawling bushes.
name: Wild Roses

desc:
Large, jagged boulders tear through the ground and mark the end of a gnarly
bush-tangled wilderness that dominates the north. The land slowly descends to
the west into a gentle hillside while the boulders continue their march
southward. Visibility is limited by the bushes that grow among the boulders.
name: Rising Boulder-strewn Hillside

desc:
The creek flows swiftly among a cluster of very large rocks that sit at the
bottom of a boulder-covered hillside, itself dotted with bushes and long-bladed
grasses. Immediately surrounding the creek are rushes and reeds that limit
visibility. Only the tops of a few trees can be seen to the south.
name: Large, Protruding Boulders

desc:
A swift creek drains into a fertile lake surrounded by rushes and reeds at the
lowest point of this valley. Grasses and wildflowers grow further up the
slightly inclined banks of the lake where the valley continues to the south. The
northern horizon is dotted with boulders as the land rises. Travelling to the
north means following the meandering creek across the path it has cut.
name: Lake in a Fertile Valley

desc:
A narrow dirt track leads eastwards towards a small farmhouse. It is straight
but somewhat uneven, dipping slightly in places before rising again, and two
well-worn cart tracks are cut into the surface. A split-rail fence runs along
the track to the south, blocking movement into the fields beyond, and messy
weeds and tall grasses grow along that edge of the track and under the fence.
North off the track are rows of crops, orderly and well-tended, while to the
west the track passes through a gap in the fence and leaves this farmstead.
name: Farm Track

desc:
A narrow dirt farm track ends here in a wide circle before the farmhouse to the
east. At the centre of the circle, a well sits surrounded by low-growing grasses
and weeds that extend out to the edges of the track before the hard-packed dirt
stops them short. The track itself is fairly smooth and level, with two shallow
ruts left from carts or wagons. Many small rocks are half-buried in the dirt
surface, and more have been pushed to the edges over time, leaving a thin gravel
edge that marks the beginning of the adjacent fields. To the south is a
split-rail fence, and to the north are well-tended crop fields leading towards a
large barn.
name: Farm Track before a Farmhouse

desc:
Long, straight rows of crops run from north to south in these fields. The crops
are sown in raised ridges of rich, dark soil; between the rows are shallow
furrows. There are weeds growing in these furrows, short but broad of leaf, and
some growing at the bases of the crops as well. In places these weeds have been
cut off at their bases and hoed under the soil, leaving only traces of stems and
cut leaves behind. A barn is visible to the north, with a broad barn door, and
to the east is a small farmhouse with a window.
name: Crop Fields before a Barn

desc:
This large wooden barn is constructed from boards nailed to a framework of
sturdy square posts. These support posts run down the centre of the barn, from
one side to the other, and rise all the way up to the middle of the peaked roof,
with crossbeams supporting them at regular intervals. The interior of this barn
is cluttered: hay takes up the west wall in a cascading pile, and junk takes up
the northern half of the barn, the detritus left from the wear and tear imposed
on the tools necessary to run a farm. Some usable tools hang from the lowest
rafters here and there, some of them rusted but otherwise in good condition. The
floor underfoot is dirt mixed with stray pieces of hay. To the east and south
large barn doors lead out to the fields beyond.
name: Spacious Barn

desc:
These overgrown fields behind the barn are fenced in to the north and east, and
blocked to the south by the north wall of the farmhouse. It looks like they were
once used as pastureland, but now appear to be unused, abandoned, and overgrown.
Tall clumps of grasses mix with small scrub bushes, thigh-high on a man, and an
old hay bale in one corner of the field is decaying where it sits, spread out
slightly and moulding at its centre. The weeds and stray bushes grow tallest
along the fence line, the split-rail fence choked by twining vines and tangled
branches that thread through its rails and wrap around its posts.
name: Back Fields

desc:
These crop fields are bordered to the west and north by a simple split-rail
fence. Beneath the fence tall weeds and messy grasses grow, but a little farther
inwards the rows of crops are neat, straight, and well-tended. Bare dirt is
exposed between the crop rows, sunk below the slightly raised ridges upon which
the crops were sown, and this bare dirt is rich and dark, albeit somewhat rocky.
Some weeds stick up in these rows and also between the crops, although they are
small: anything larger seems to have been hoed under, leaving only small traces
of chopped stems and leaves. To the south a dirt road runs towards a farmhouse
visible in the distance to the southeast.
name: Rows of Crops

desc:
Hard shrubs cling on southerly sides of the larger stones that are scattered
here. East of here, a bald scarp sharply rises, halting any travel in that
direction. Otherwise the plain is unbroken in all directions. Vegetation grows
thickly to the west, preventing travel in that direction.
name: Bleak Plain

desc:
This land transitions from a damp land to the west, gradually becoming drier to
the east. Birch and brushwood grow thickly to the west, and a row of yellow
buttercup grow next to them. Gradually the flora thins east where grasses and
shrub give way to gorse.
name: Transitioning Lands

desc:
Northwest here, grasses overtake the birch and brushwood that dominate the
western edge of this plain. The grass grows thick here, hiding insects and small
animals beneath its depths. Some small shrubs sparsely decorate the land, bent
and scraggly, and dirt swirls in the air.
name: Transitioning Plains

desc:
These small rolling hills are peaceful, the ground being covered in golden
grasses that blow about mesmerizingly. The vista provides views in all
directions, allowing little cover. To the west a sharp scarp impedes travel.
name: Peaceful Vista

desc:
Foothills roll over land covered by golden grasses. No other plant seems to be
able to withstand the dogged northern gales. To the south, the land drops
sharply in a cliff where it overlooks a creek bed. Animal skeletons litter the
land just before the eastern cliff, site of an animal feast. A path leads to the
east.
name: Foothills

desc:
A peak in the grassy, rolling hills stands high above tent roofs and the husks
of aged buildings that appear between the undulating hills below. Scattered
about the peak are small shrubs and stunted deciduous trees. A steep path winds
down the hill to the east and south.
name: Rolling Hills above a Ruined Camp

desc:
The land here lies lower than the surrounding hills to the north and west, and
is surrounded on the east and south by brown leather tents. A tall, mossy pine
lives south of the path, its branches providing a high perch for owls,
squirrels, and snakes.
name: Fork in a Grassy Path

desc:
Blackthorn forms a thick meandering copse at the bottom of this hilly gap. The
low lying land is obviously a creek bed, but its water depth is nearly
impossible to tell, with all the brush choking the top. The bed runs south,
heading into an even more jammed shrub-gully.
name: Creek Gap

desc:
Tall cliff walls impose the land north and west, scrub pine cramming themselves
on the rocky cliffs. Water stains indicate a few trickles which feed the brushy
plants at the bottom of this gully. Running south is a creekbed, choked with
lush blackthorn. Club moss covers any exposed rock, and the sounds of trickling
water permeate the surroundings.
name: Brushy Creekbed

desc:
Steep cliffs loom over both sides of this wild gully, blocking out all but
midday light. Brambles grow thick and prickly in a clump on the southestern edge
of the gully turn. A small pool can be seen shining just in front of the bend,
choked with grass. Rocks intermix with mud and brush along the creekbed.
name: Wild Gully

desc:
The ground here is rocky and steep, inclined north between the scarped walls of
this gully. Little light gets here and the creekbed is well defined, as few
plants are able to survive but clubmoss. The walls of the gully are cut of stone
and are stained dark rust from water seeping down the sides.
name: Steep-walled Gully

desc:
Brisk air blows from the north, bending what little plant life that exists to
its will. The hills are low and strewn with prickly brush and bent pine. They
continue northeast, gradually rising to form a larger range. Directly north of
here the land ends in a steep scarped drop off.
name: Bleak Hills

desc:
These foothills are thicketed with brambles and shrubs, intertwined with short
grasses and large round stones. The landscape forms a natural maze, traversable
by small animals with ease. A stiff northerly breeze is constantly sweeping
overland, stirring up dirt and leaves in little dust devils.
name: Thicketed Hills

desc:
A grassy path here is shaded by many branches of pines and elms. Roots from the
trees crisscross the path, creating tiny hills and a home for insects and small
animals. To the north the landscape rises to a peak, and the path continues
southward. Exploration east and west is obstructed by tall rocky hills.
name: Grassy Path between Hills

desc:
A simple lean-to has been constructed along the back of this farmhouse, a
continuation of the roof that slopes down at a lesser angle until it ends at
support posts that have been driven deep into the bare dirt. A large wood pile
has been stacked underneath the lean-to, near the back wall of the house but not
against it, and a large stump with an axe sunk into it stands a short distance
away. Various farm tools are hung on pegs on the back wall of the house by the
wood pile, protected from the elements by the roof above. A simple wooden porch,
no more than a few short planks laid together, leads to the back door of the
house.
name: Lean-to behind a Farmhouse

desc:
Viewing holes cut into the black leather wall provide a limited view of hills to
the west. Wooden chairs are placed in front of the holes, creating a primitive
lookout spot in this guard post. The air inside the tent smells of stale beer
and sweat.
name: Guard Post in Some Rolling Hills

desc:
A turn in the hill here creates a natural windbreak, and a open-air storage tent
has been constructed into it out of aged brown leather. Wooden provision crates
are stacked neatly along the perimeter, and leather-wrapped poles prop up the
sides. The ground here is a jumbled mix of pebbles, weeds, and patches of grass.
To the north and east, a grassy path begins.
name: Open Storage Tent

desc:
The hills to the north and south create a natural crease for a grassy path. The
grass here has been kept short, and shows moderate use. To the west lies an
open-aired storage tent, and the grassy pocket of a path continues eastward.
name: East of an Open Storage Tent

desc:
The grassy path here is straddled on the north and south by tall hills. A
broken, rocky, and nondescript stub of an ancient building lies on the southern
hill. Within it a cluster of flower patches and shrubs have taken residence. The
grassy path continues east and west.
name: Decaying Ruins Amongst Rolling Hills

desc:
Tall green hills to the north and east guide the grassy path west and southward.
A partially buried and cracked stone slab leans against a giant bay tree. Faint
lines chiselled into it form a human warrior with an oversized shield and sword.
name: Turn in a Grassy Path

desc:
The trampled, muddy, and rooty ground here shows signs of heavy use. To the
east, a large brown leather tent is nestled within overgrown grass. A cracked
skull is stitched into its skin using white leather strips, and its open doorway
flutters in the breeze. To the north, a grassy path begins, and tall hills
southward prevent further travel.
name: Before a Large Tent

desc:
Heavy use of this muddy path have exposed roots and subsurface rocks. The path
forks here east-west and south. A mighty elm grows at the peak of the hill to
the north. Thick green moss forms a patchy carpet on its bark, and twin vines
dance up the tree.
name: Trampled Fork amongst Some Ruins

desc:
A thin veneer of grass winds its way east and west between decaying ruins to the
north and south. Leaves, twigs and stones have collected at the bottom of a
small depression here. Broken and twisted roots peek out from the sides of it. A
tall and thorn-covered leather tent sits to the south. Two moneybags are crudely
painted onto the side of it.
name: Dip between Decaying Ruins

desc:
The grass and vegetation here is sparse, indicating a well-trodden path. A
towering, ancient oak provides shade and a home to a variety of fauna. A large,
black leather tent is concealed within a decimated stone structure to the south.
Two parallel swords are etched into its exterior with thin animal bones. To the
east and west, the dirt path continues between the hills.
name: Before a Fortified Tent

desc:
Wooden tables and benches line the perimeter of this mess hall. In the centre
sits a simple serving table. Dried crimson forms a patchwork of stains across
the tables, and the meatless bones of mutton and sheep lie scattered about the
floor. On the serving table sits a neat stack of wooden plates, forks, and
spoons. Two hanging oil lamps provide shaky light to the room. A dirt path
starts to the west.
name: Mess Hall

desc:
Faded tan leather forms the walls of this room, and light from the sun and stars
stream through ripped patches of ceiling in this simple watering hole. Small
discarded bones are embedded in the dirt floor. A deep well opens in the corner,
the mud deep around it.
name: Damp Watering Hole

desc:
Vegetation here is sparse with heavy use of the trail. An open-air tent used as
crude barracks sits among tumbled ruins to the west. The trail becomes more
obscured to the south, as shrubs and tall grass compete for sunlight, and
continues north and south.
name: Meeting of Two Well-trodden Paths

desc:
The landscape rises to the north and south, creating a natural path in this
small valley. Short and angled grass in the path here indicates moderate use.
Thick roots run parallel to the ground, and small shoots struggle to survive the
onslaught of constant foot traffic.
name: Well-trodden Path in Rolling Hills

desc:
The branching path here is faint amidst the thick vegetation. To the north and
east, the path continues amongst the hills. To the south, a hitching post is
fastened to a large boulder. The branches of an ancient oak reach overhead from
the east, providing a small amount of respite from the sun, rain, and wind. The
ground here is covered with the fall leavings from the tree.
name: Split in a Faint Path

desc:
A place of equine respite and watering is fastened by leather straps and a metal
anchor to a large rock on the western hillside here. Muddy ground surrounds a
wooden water trowel under the hitching post. Hoof prints and animal hair are
embedded within the watery ground. Large stone formations and a large black tent
prohibit further travel east, and the path becomes less descript to the south.
name: Hitching Post

desc:
The dense foliage here partially obscures a dirt path that leads north and east.
To the south a decaying tower sits atop a small hill, its cracked columns
decorated with centuries of ivy and moss growth. The top of the tower has
partially collapsed, leaving a ragged gash in the stonework.
name: North of a Decaying Tower

desc:
A fortified tent sits amidst crumbling rocky ruins to the north. Large boulders
surround the tent's black leather walls, forming a primitive sort of protection
from the elements and adversaries. The east-west path here is partially obscured
by dense undergrowth. Thick branches of a tall oak sway in the breeze overhead.
name: South of a Fortified Tent

desc:
An obscured trail continues here east and west. Thick roots peek up from the
ground, only to quickly disappear again into the underbrush. A tall tent,
covered in a carpet of thorns as a primitive warning to outsiders, sits to the
north. A rocky hill rises from the valley to the south.
name: Path through Overgrown Foliage

desc:
The hills surrounding this small valley guide an overgrown path west and
northward. Thick shrubs and tall grass obscure the winding path below. Patches
of aeglos, heather, and iris grow on the hillside to the south. The path
continues to the north and west.
name: Turn in an Overgrown Path

desc:
Simple wooden cots, lined with thick leather and animal hides, line the
perimeter of these open-air barracks. The tan leather walls on three sides offer
limited protection from the outside elements. A table sits in the centre of the
tent, and discarded mugs of ale, rotten pilfered purses, and animal bones litter
the dirt floor. Two poles wrapped in black leather provide structural support
for the entrance.
name: Airy Garrison

desc:
The leather tent's oiled walls glisten in the dim light provided by a large oil
lamp. Two leather hammocks on the eastern wall provide a respite from the damp
dirt floor below. Directly opposite them is a heavy oak table, adorned with
paper maps of the surrounding areas, writings, and drawings.
name: Fortified Tent

desc:
Smells of earth and decay permeate this cellar. The mud here is deep and marked
by tiny trails, no doubt left by insects and small rodents. The walls look to
have been hastily dug, as their angles are not straight. Tiny rivulets of water
run from a small hole in the eastern wall, feeding the deep mud.
name: Muddy Excavation

desc:
Large boulders, leaves and broken branches lie scattered on the muddy ground
here. Stone spires and a cracked staircase form the decayed skeleton of an
ancient tower. The circular staircase winds its way along the tower's perimeter
as it heads upward. The aged spires and open roof above offer little protection
from the outside elements.
name: Base of a Decaying Tower

desc:
The circular staircase ends here at a small stone landing. The stone roof has
been decimated by time, and large portions of it can be seen strewn about the
floor below. Ancient vines wind their way lazily up the spires. The walls here
rise high above the landing, offering protection from the wind, but blocking the
view in all directions. On the landing is a jumbled mix of large boulders,
perhaps a ruined staircase to the top of the ramparts.
name: Top of a Decaying Tower

desc:
Low rolling foothills stretch as far as the eye can see, grass blowing golden on
the highland. A large bowled brake of cedar trees is the discerning view, it
writhes and swirls as the north wind blows over sending aromatic scents about.
name: Cedar Brake

desc:
Shallow rolling hills spread across the land, covered in tufts of golden grass.
Dotting the ground are large granite boulders, grey and imposing, strewn about
as if dropped from the sky. The boulders seem formidable, but upon further
perusal the northern faces are smooth, worn down by velocious winds.
name: Boulder-strewn Hills

desc:
The walls of this cavern, several yards apart, are smoothly cut into the earth,
likely the result of rainfall and erosion. Several short tree roots have
tunneled their way through the rock, and into the open air of the cave. The
floor is composed mostly of packed dirt, and black rock.
name: Wide Cavern

desc:
The dirt walls that form this cramped pit lie only a couple yards from each
other, making this hole an unlikely home for large animals. The walls are
covered with the ragged, bent stubs of tree roots, and the air is thick with the
smell of damp earth. Boulders poke their way from the earth, and several small
pubbles lay scattered about the uneven ground.
name: Cramped Pit

desc:
A low boulder hangs over a large entrance that leads into a rough cave. Inside,
the stone walls and ceiling are cragged and dry to the touch. A level floor is
covered with a mix of dirt, bark, and leaves, providing a cushioning that gives
the den quiet warmth. A slightly musty smell floats about, a display of the
beasts that live here.
name: Bear Den

desc:
This small farmhouse is simply constructed, with log walls and wood plank floors
and a peaked roof with crossbeams. This part of the farmhouse serves as both a
kitchen and a living or dining area, with a table and low benches in the centre
and a cast iron wood-burning stove set against the southern wall that leads up
to a chimney. A large wash basin by the stove sits next to a table and some
shelves, which contain jars of flour and sugar and preserved vegetables. To the
north an open doorway leads into what appears to be a bedroom, while doors to
the east and west lead out of the house.
name: Simple Farmhouse

desc:
This small bedroom in the farmhouse has wood slat floors and log walls that lead
up to a peaked roof with exposed crossbeams. A single window is set in the
western wall, looking out over the crop fields maintained by the farm, but
otherwise the walls of this room are unadorned. A single narrow bed with a straw
mattress sits against the northern wall, next to a washbasin on a small table.
To the south an open doorway leads back to the main room of the house.
name: Small Bedroom in a Farmhouse

desc:
Well-cultivated fields replete with evidence of skilful farming surround this
intersection in the dirt road, which is wide enough for a cart to pass safely.
Beyond the fields, an old moss-covered and crumbling stone wall marks the
boundary between the cultivated land and the surrounding wilderness. In places
where the wall has fallen, a wooden fence has been erected to patch the holes
and keep out wildlife. The track continues to the east and south.
name: Intersection between Fields

desc:
To the west, verdant fields overflowing with cultivated crops eventually give
way to the wilderness beyond the old stone wall, interrupted only by the
well-maintained dirt road that cuts between them. A house has been contructed
into the hillside in the north. The house's front door lies to the east. A tidy
stone path travails the short distance north from the road to a heavy cellar
door. Along the broken stone wall at the farm's eastern boundary, a rough path
determinedly trudges south toward a distant pond.
name: Overflowing Field of Grain

desc:
The road ends here near the top of a grassy knoll. Various herbs grow in raised
beds on either side of a flagstone path leading to a sturdy wooden door set into
a farmhouse to the north. The dirt road, wide enough for a cart, continues back
down the slope to the west. To the south and east the terrain drops steeply to
the flat fields below.
name: Verdant Herb Garden

desc:
Fruit trees in tidy rows grow here, bordered on the southern and western sides
by the quiet ruins of an old stone wall. The dirt road that ends here, wide
enough for a cart, runs north through fertile fields toward the entrance to the
farm. To the east, the orchard ends and the grasses grow taller. Insects and
small animals interrupt the stillness.
name: Orchard behind Well-kept Fields

desc:
Cultivated fields to the north and west give way first to tall grasses and
forbs, and then finally to rushes and reeds along the edge of an old pond. The
clear water provides a habitat for insects, amphibians, and water-fowl that can
be observed among the algae and the tops of plants growing from the mud. Near
the pond, the ground underfoot is soft with water.
name: Verdant Pond

desc:
This farm house is framed by great wooden beams and whitewashed walls that are
set with wattle and daub. It is very warm and inviting, modestly furnished, and
kept tidy by the owners. A small window peeks out into a herb garden and a
narrow wooden staircase in the corner leads down to the cellar.
name: Main Room of a Farm House

desc:
The walls and floor of this rectangular room are made from stone blocks, and
great horizontal wooden beams hold up a wooden ceiling. Wooden shelves are
mounted on the walls and many items have been stored on them. A narrow wooden
staircase leads up to a hatch in the ceiling.
name: Cool Cellar

desc:
A tight hole is slick with mud and wet rocks, water coming in from a hole
overhead. The hole slides down and north, with loose rocks stopping travellers
from slipping haphazard to another, wetter drop. The air is rich with the smell
of dirt and blows up cool and gentle from below.
name: Narrow Sinkhole

desc:
A slick, muddy sinkhole widens as it pitches sharply down, spotted with enough
rocks to provide a meagre passage. Water trickles through the mud in never
ending rivulets, giving the cavern a state of tumult. The air is cold, and
sounds of squeaks and flutters can be heard in the distance.
name: Widening Sinkhole

desc:
A path has been cleared through a muddy sinkhole and leads to a steep, walkable
slope up and south. At the bottom of the hole is a large accumulation of stones,
wet and packed thick. The ceiling cuts down low and muddy on the north end,
opening up abruptly just past the stones. About, the air is cold and wet.
name: Sinkhole

desc:
Long stalactites hang down wet-orange in this immense cavern, and along the
north side are large enough to reach waiting stalagmites. Damp, water beads on
all surfaces, dripping and gliding down to the craggy floor. The air is cool and
full of echoing squeaks. To the east, the cave narrows to a tunnel.
name: Rough, Natural Cave

desc:
The back of this cave smells of human filth and rotting cloth, and is even
wetter than the front. Crammed at the far wall are bones, rotten potatoes, fur
scraps, offal, torn clothes and broken weapons. The floor tilts to the back, and
a bit of foul water can be seen pooled below the garbage.
name: Back of a Natural Cave

desc:
Slick, wet walls slope down to a narrow floor in this rough and pockmarked
tunnel. The ceiling is high as the tunnel bends north, and becomes lower as it
turns to the west, just above the height of a man. Sounds of water dripping from
above into small pools echo quietly about, and the smell of clay is deep.
name: Winding, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
A winding, narrow tunnel extends west, widening to a vast cave. South, the
tunnel narrows and large blocks of stone cause the path to tighten in three
spots. The ground is natural, uneven, rough and muddy, slowing traffic to all
but the most natural of cave dweller. The walls on either side slant up and
extend to a tall ceiling that has actively dripping stalactites.
name: Winding, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Sounds echo in this large, tall-ceilinged cavern. The floor is uneven, with two
larger high spots of lumpy rock. A depression in the eastern part of the cave is
covered in pelts, cookware and a recently used roasting spit. Most of the ground
is bare rock, with only corners or low spots filled with mud. On the western
wall, high up, is a ledge that leads treacherously over to a narrow, vertical
crevice on the north wall.
name: Spacious, Natural Cavern

desc:
Behind some roots is a rocky ledge, hard and smooth, appearing well used. The
ledge is set high upon the wall of a large cave, and provides a good view of the
great distance that would be travelled if one fell from here. Close inspection
of the walls reveal rocky knobs that stick out at opportune spaces. To the west
an acrid smell emanates, evident from the brown, foul dirt that oozes out from
under the tips of the roots.
name: Ledge behind Some Roots

desc:
This narrow crevice extends upwards to the top of a tall ceiling. The walls are
rough rock and slick with moisture, extending up from the narrowest point at the
floor. As the crevice widens, a larger space is evident some thirty feet up, and
could fit two men hidden from the cave below.
name: Narrow, Vertical Crevice

desc:
Sour, vaporous odours fill this small alcove. The floor is mucky with large,
mushy piles of guano and gives to every step. Squeaks and flutters fill the room
with incessant noises as roots hang down, thick and grey on the east side.
name: Narrow, Guano-filled Alcove
